text.NRSV: All wisdom is from the Lord, and with him it remains forever.
text.DRB: All wisdom is from the Lord God, and hath been always with him, and is before all time.
text.KJV: 1:1 This Iesus was the sonne of Sirach, and grand-childe to Iesus of the same name with him; This man therefore liued in the latter times, after the people had bene led away captiue, and called home againe, and almost after all the Prophets. Now his grandfather Iesus (as he himselfe witnesseth) was a man of great diligence and wisedome among the Hebrewes, who did not onely gather the graue and short Sentences of wise men, that had bene before him, but himselfe also vttered some of his owne, full of much vnderstanding and wisedome. When as therefore the first Iesus died, leauing this booke almost perfected, Si rach his sonne receiuing it after him, left it to his owne sonne Iesus, who hauing gotten it into his hands, compiled it all orderly into one Volume, and called it Wisdome, Intituling it, both by his owne name, his fathers name, and his grandfathers, alluring the hearer by the very name of Wisedome, to haue a greater loue to the studie of this Booke. It conteineth therefore wise Sayings, darke Sentences, and Parables, and certaine particular ancient godly stories of men that pleased God. Also his Prayer and Song. Moreouer, what benefits God had vouchsafed his people, and what plagues he had heaped vpon their enemies. This Iesus did imitate Solomon, and was no lesse famous for Wisedome, and learning, both being indeed a man of great learning, and so reputed also. Whereas many and great things haue bene deliuered vnto vs by the Law and the Prophets, and by others that haue followed their steps, for the which things Israel ought to be commended for learning and Wisedome, and whereof not onely the Readers must needs become skilful themselues, but also they that desire to learne, be able to profit them which are without, both by speaking and writing : My grandfather Iesus, when he had much giuen himselfe to the reading of the Law, and the Prophets, and other Bookes of our fathers, and had gotten therein good iudgement, was drawen on also himselfe, to write something pertayning to learning and Wisedome, to the intent that those which are desirous to learne, and are addicted to these things, might profit much more in liuing according to the Law. Wherefore, let me intreat you to reade it with fauour and attention, and to pardon Vs, wherein wee may secme to come short of some words which we haue laboured to interprete. For the same things vttered in Hebrew, and translated into an other tongue, haue not the same force in them : and not onely these things, but the Law it selfe, and the Prophets, and the rest of the Bookes, haue no small difference, when they are spoken in their owne language. For in the eight and thirtieth yeere comming into Egypt, when Euergetes was King, and continuing there some time, I found a Booke of no small learning, therefore I thought it most necessary for mee, to bestow some diligence and trauaile to interprete it: Vsing great watchfulnesse, and skill in that space, to bring the Booke to an end, and set it foorth for them also, which in a strange countrey are willing to learne, being prepared before in maners to line after the Law. All wisedome commeth from the Lord, and is with him for euer.

text.NRSV: The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity—who can count them?
text.DRB: Who hath numbered the sand of the sea, and the drops of rain, and the days of the world? Who hath measured the height of heaven, and the breadth of the earth, and the depth of the abyss?
text.KJV: 1:2 Who can number the sand of the sea, and the drops of raine, and the dayes of eternity?

text.NRSV: The height of heaven, the breadth of the earth, the abyss, and wisdom—who can search them out?
text.DRB: Who hath searched out the wisdom of God that goeth before all things?
text.KJV: 1:3 Who can finde out the height of heauen, and the breadth of the earth, and the deepe, and wisedome?

text.NRSV: Wisdom was created before all other things, and prudent understanding from eternity.
text.DRB: Wisdom hath been created before all things, and the understanding of prudence from everlasting.
text.KJV: 1:4 Wisedome hath beene created before all things, and the vnderstanding of prudence from euerlasting.

text.DRB: The word of God on high is the fountain of wisdom, and her ways are everlasting commandments.
text.KJV: 1:5 The word of God most high, is the fountaine of wisdome, & her wayes are euerlasting commandements.

text.NRSV: The root of wisdom—to whom has it been revealed? Her subtleties—who knows them?
text.DRB: To whom hath the root of wisdom been revealed, and who hath known her wise counsels?
text.KJV: 1:6 To whom hath the root of wisdome beene reuealed? or who hath knowen her wise counsels?

text.DRB: To whom hath the discipline of wisdom been revealed and made manifest? and who hath understood the multiplicity of her steps?

text.NRSV: There is but one who is wise, greatly to be feared, seated upon his throne—the Lord.
text.DRB: There is one most high Creator Almighty, and a powerful king, and greatly to be feared, who sitteth upon his throne, and is the God of dominion.
text.KJV: 1:8 There is one wise and greatly to bee feared; the Lord sitting vpon his Throne.

text.NRSV: It is he who created her; he saw her and took her measure; he poured her out upon all his works,
text.DRB: He created her in the Holy Ghost, and saw her, and numbered her, and measured her.
text.KJV: 1:9 He created her, and saw her, and numbred her, and powred her out vpon all his workes.

text.NRSV: upon all the living according to his gift; he lavished her upon those who love him.
text.DRB: And he poured her out upon all his works, and upon all flesh according to his gift, and hath given her to them that love him.
text.KJV: 1:10 Shee with all flesh according to his gift, and hee hath giuen her to them that loue him.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is glory and exultation, and gladness and a crown of rejoicing.
text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is honour, and glory, and gladness, and a crown of joy.
text.KJV: 1:11 The feare of the Lord is honour, and glory, and gladnesse, and a crowne of reioycing.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord delights the heart, and gives gladness and joy and long life.
text.DRB: The fear of the Lord shall delight the heart, and shall give joy, and gladness, and length of days.
text.KJV: 1:12 The feare of the Lord maketh a merrie heart, and giueth ioy and gladnesse, and a long life.

text.DRB: With him that feareth the Lord, it shall go well in the latter end, and in the day of his death he shall be blessed.
text.KJV: 1:13 Who so feareth the Lord, it shall goe well with him at the last, & he shall finde fauour in the day of his death.

text.NRSV: To fear the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; she is created with the faithful in the womb.
text.DRB: The love of God is honourable wisdom.
text.KJV: 1:14 To feare the Lord, is the beginning of wisedome: and it was created with the faithfull in the wombe.

text.DRB: And they to whom she shall shew herself love her by the sight, and by the knowledge of her great works.
text.KJV: 1:15 Shee hath built an euerlasting foundation with men, and she shal continue with their seede.

text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and was created with the faithful in the womb, it walketh with chosen women, and is known with the just and faithful.
text.KJV: 1:16 To feare the Lord, is fulnesse of wisedome, and filleth men with her fruits.

text.DRB: The fear of the Lord is the religiousness of knowledge.
text.KJV: 1:17 Shee filleth all their house with things desireable, and the garners with her increase.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the crown of wisdom, making peace and perfect health to flourish.
text.DRB: Religiousness shall keep and justify the heart, it shall give joy and gladness.
text.KJV: 1:18 The feare of the Lord is a crowne of wisedome, making peace and perfect health to flourish, both which are the gifts of God: and it enlargeth their reioycing that loue him.

text.Base: To fear the Lord is the root of wisdom, and her branches are long life.
text.KJV: 1:20 The root of wisedome is to feare the Lord, and the branches thereof are long life.

text.Base: Unjust anger cannot be justified, for anger tips the scale to one’s ruin.
text.KJV: 1:22 A furious man cannot be iustified, for the sway of his fury shalbe his destruction.

text.Base: If you desire wisdom, keep the commandments, and the Lord will lavish her upon you.
text.KJV: 1:26 If thou desire wisedome, keepe the commaundements, and the Lord shall giue her vnto thee.

text.Base: For the fear of the Lord is wisdom and discipline; faithfulness and humility are his delight.
text.KJV: 1:27 For the feare of the Lord is wisdome, and instruction: and faith and meekenesse are his delight.

text.Base: Do not disobey the fear of the Lord; do not approach him with a divided mind.
text.KJV: 1:28 Distrust not the feare of the Lord when thou art poore: and come not vnto him with a double heart.

text.Base: Do not exalt yourself, or you may fall and bring dishonor upon yourself.
text.KJV: 1:30 Exalt not thy selfe, lest thou fall, and bring dishonor vpon thy soule, and so God discouer thy secrets, and cast thee downe in the midst of the congregation, because thou camest not in trueth, to the feare of the Lord: but thy heart is full of deceit.

text.Base: My child, when you come to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for testing.
text.KJV: 2:1 My sonne, if thou come to serue the Lorde, prepare thy soule for temptation.

text.Base: Set your heart right and be steadfast, and do not be impetuous in time of calamity.
text.KJV: 2:2 Set thy heart aright, and constantly endure, and make not haste in time of trouble.

text.Base: Cling to him and do not depart, so that your last days may be prosperous.
text.KJV: 2:3 Cleaue vnto him, and depart not away, that thou mayest be increased at thy last end.

text.Base: Accept whatever befalls you, and in times of humiliation be patient.
text.KJV: 2:4 Whatsoeuer is brought vpon thee, take cheerefully, and bee patient when thou art changed to a lowe estate.

text.Base: For gold is tested in the fire, and those found acceptable, in the furnace of humiliation.
text.KJV: 2:5 For gold is tried in the fire, and acceptable men in the furnace of aduersitie.

text.Base: Trust in him, and he will help you; make your ways straight, and hope in him.
text.KJV: 2:6 Beleeue in him, and he will helpe thee, order thy way aright, and trust in him.

text.Base: You who fear the Lord, wait for his mercy; do not stray, or you may fall.
text.KJV: 2:7 Ye that feare the Lord, waite for his mercie, and goe not aside, lest ye fall.

text.Base: You who fear the Lord, trust in him, and your reward will not be lost.
text.KJV: 2:8 Yee that feare the Lord, beleeue him, and your reward shall not faile.

text.Base: You who fear the Lord, hope for good things, for lasting joy and mercy.
text.KJV: 2:9 Ye that feare the Lord, hope for good, and for euerlasting ioy and mercy.

text.Base: Consider the ancient generations and see: who ever trusted in the Lord and was put to shame? Or who ever persevered in the fear of the Lord and was forsaken? Or who ever called upon him and was overlooked?
text.KJV: 2:10 Looke at the generations of old, and see, did euer any trust in the Lord, and was confounded? or did any abide in his feare, & was forsaken? or whom did hee euer despise, that called vpon him?

text.Base: For the Lord is compassionate and merciful; he forgives sins and saves in time of distress.
text.KJV: 2:11 For the Lord is full of compassion, and mercie, longsuffering, and very pitifull, and forgiueth sinnes, and saueth in time of affliction.

text.Base: Woe to timid hearts and to slack hands, and to the sinner who walks a double path!
text.KJV: 2:12 Woe be to fearefull hearts, and faint hands, and the sinner that goeth two wayes.

text.Base: Woe to the fainthearted who have no trust! Therefore they will have no shelter.
text.KJV: 2:13 Woe vnto him that is faint hearted, for he beleeueth not, therefore shall he not be defended.

text.Base: Woe to you who have lost your nerve! What will you do when the Lord’s reckoning comes?
text.KJV: 2:14 Woe vnto you that haue lost patience: and what will ye doe when the Lord shall visite you?

text.Base: Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his words, and those who love him keep his ways.
text.KJV: 2:15 They that feare the Lord, will not disobey his word, and they that loue him, will keepe his wayes.

text.Base: Those who fear the Lord prepare their hearts, and humble themselves before him.
text.KJV: 2:17 They that feare the Lord, will prepare their hearts, and humble their soules in his sight:

text.Base: Let us fall into the hands of the Lord, but not into the hands of mortals; for equal to his majesty is his mercy, and equal to his name are his works.
text.KJV: 2:18 Saying, We will fal into the hands of the Lord, and not into the hands of men: for as his maiestie is, so is his mercie.

text.Base: Listen to me your father, O children; act accordingly, that you may be kept in safety.
text.KJV: 3:1 Heare mee your father, O children, and doe thereafter, that ye may be safe.

text.Base: For the Lord honors a father above his children, and he confirms a mother’s right over her sons.
text.KJV: 3:2 For the Lord hath giuen the father honour ouer the children, and hath confirmed the authoritie of the mother ouer the sonnes.

text.Base: Those who honor their father atone for sins,
text.KJV: 3:3 Who so honoureth his father, maketh an atonement for his sinnes.

text.Base: and those who respect their mother are like those who lay up treasure.
text.KJV: 3:4 And he that honoureth his mother, is as one that layeth vp treasure.

text.Base: Honor your father by word and deed, that his blessing may come upon you.
text.KJV: 3:8 Honour thy father and mother, both in word and deed, that a blessing may come vpon thee from them.

text.Base: For a father’s blessing strengthens the houses of the children, but a mother’s curse uproots their foundations.
text.KJV: 3:9 For the blessing of the father establisheth the houses of children, but the curse of the mother rooteth out foundations.

text.Base: My child, help your father in his old age, and do not grieve him as long as he lives;
text.KJV: 3:12 My sonne, helpe thy father in his age, and grieue him not as long as hee liueth.

text.Base: even if his mind fails, be patient with him; because you have all your faculties do not despise him.
text.KJV: 3:13 And if his vnderstanding faile, haue patience with him, and despise him not, when thou art in thy ful strength.

text.Base: My child, perform your tasks with humility; then you will be loved by those whom God accepts.
text.KJV: 3:17 My sonne, goe on with thy businesse in meekenesse, so shalt thou be beloued of him that is approued.

text.Base: The greater you are, the more you must humble yourself; so you will find favor in the sight of the Lord.
text.KJV: 3:18 The greater thou art, the more humble thy selfe, and thou shalt find fauour before the Lord.

text.Base: Neither seek what is too difficult for you, nor investigate what is beyond your power.
text.KJV: 3:21 Seeke not out the things that are too hard for thee, neither search the things that are aboue thy strength.

text.Base: Reflect upon what has been assigned to you, for you do not need what is hidden.
text.KJV: 3:22 But what is commaunded thee, thinke thereupon with reuerence, for it is not needfull for thee, to see with thine eyes, the things that are in secret.

text.Base: A stubborn mind will fare badly at the end, and whoever loves danger will perish in it.
text.KJV: 3:26 A stubborne heart shall fare euill at the last, and he that loueth danger shall perish therein.

text.Base: As water extinguishes a blazing fire, so almsgiving atones for sin.
text.KJV: 3:30 Water will quench a flaming fire, and almes maketh an attonement for sinnes.

text.Base: My child, do not cheat the poor of their living, and do not keep needy eyes waiting.
text.KJV: 4:1 My sonne, defraude not the poore of his liuing, and make not the needy eies to waite long.

text.Base: Do not grieve the hungry, or anger one in need.
text.KJV: 4:2 Make not an hungry soule sorrowfull, neither prouoke a man in his distresse.

text.Base: For if in bitterness of soul some should curse you, their Creator will hear their prayer.
text.KJV: 4:6 For if he curse thee in the bitternesse of his soule, his prayer shall be heard of him that made him.

text.Base: Endear yourself to the congregation; bow your head low to the great.
text.KJV: 4:7 Get thy selfe the loue of the congregation, and bow thy head to a great man.

text.Base: Give a hearing to the poor, and return their greeting politely.
text.KJV: 4:8 Let it not grieue thee to bowe downe thine eare to the poore, and giue him a friendly answere with meekenesse.

text.Base: Be like a father to orphans, and like a husband to their mother; you will then be like a son of the Most High, and he will love you more than your mother does.
text.KJV: 4:10 Be as a father vnto the fatherlesse, and in stead of a husband vnto their mother, so shalt thou be as the sonne of the most high, and he shall loue thee more then thy mother doeth.

text.Base: Wisdom teaches her children and gives help to those who seek her.
text.KJV: 4:11 Wisedome exalteth her children, and layeth hold of them that seeke her.

text.Base: Whoever loves her loves life, and those who seek her from early morning are filled with joy.
text.KJV: 4:12 He that loueth her, loueth life, and they that seeke to her earely, shall be filled with ioy.

text.Base: For at first she will walk with them on tortuous paths; she will bring fear and dread upon them, and will torment them by her discipline until she trusts them.
text.KJV: 4:17 For at the first she will walke with him by crooked wayes, and bring feare and dread vpon him, and torment him with her discipline, vntill she may trust his soule, and try him by her Lawes.

text.Base: Watch for the opportune time, and beware of evil, and do not be ashamed to be yourself.
text.KJV: 4:20 Obserue the opportunitie, and beware of euill, and be not ashamed when it concerneth thy soule.

text.Base: For there is a shame that leads to sin, and there is a shame that is glory and favor.
text.KJV: 4:21 For there is a shame that bringeth sinne, and there is a shame which is glorie and grace.

text.Base: Do not be ashamed to confess your sins, and do not try to stop the current of a river.
text.KJV: 4:26 Bee not ashamed to confesse thy sinnes, and force not the course of the riuer.

text.Base: Fight to the death for truth, and the Lord God will fight for you.
text.KJV: 4:28 Striue for the trueth vnto death, and the Lord shall fight for thee.

text.Base: Do not rely on your wealth, or say, “I have enough.”
text.KJV: 5:1 Set not thy heart vpon thy goods, and say not, I haue ynough for my life.

text.Base: Do not follow your inclination and strength in pursuing the desires of your heart.
text.KJV: 5:2 Folow not thine owne minde, and thy strength, to walke in the wayes of thy heart:

text.Base: Do not say, “I sinned, yet what has happened to me?” for the Lord is slow to anger.
text.KJV: 5:4 Say not, I haue sinned, and what harme hath happened vnto mee? for the Lord is long suffering, he wil in no wise let thee goe.

text.Base: Do not be so confident of forgiveness that you add sin to sin.
text.KJV: 5:5 Concerning propitiation, bee not without feare to adde sinne vnto sinne.

text.Base: Do not delay to return to the Lord, and do not postpone it from day to day;
text.KJV: 5:7 Make no tarying to turne to the Lord, and put not off from day to day: for suddenly shal the wrath of the Lord come foorth, and in thy securitie thou shalt be destroyed, and perish in the day of vengeance.

text.Base: for suddenly the wrath of the Lord will come upon you, and at the time of punishment you will perish.
text.KJV: 5:8 Set not thy heart vpon goods vniustly gotten: for they shall not profit thee in the day of calamitie.

text.Base: Be steadfast in your understanding, and let your speech be consistent.
text.KJV: 5:10 Be stedfast in thy vnderstanding, and let thy word be the same.

text.Base: Be quick to hear, but be deliberate in answering.
text.KJV: 5:11 Be swift to heare, and let thy life be sincere, & with patience giue answere.

text.Base: If you know what to say, answer your neighbor; but if not, put your hand over your mouth.
text.KJV: 5:12 If thou hast vnderstanding, answer thy neighbour, if not, lay thy hand vpon thy mouth.

text.Base: Honor and dishonor come from speaking, and the tongue of mortals may be their downfall.
text.KJV: 5:13 Honour and shame is in talke; and the tongue of man is his fall.

text.Base: Pleasant speech multiplies friends, and a gracious tongue multiplies courtesies.
text.KJV: 6:5 Sweet language will multiply friends: and a faire speaking tongue will increase kinde greetings.

text.Base: Let those who are at peace with you be many, but let your advisers be one in a thousand.
text.KJV: 6:6 Be in peace with many: neuerthelesse haue but one counseller of a thousand.

text.Base: When you gain friends, gain them through testing, and do not trust them hastily.
text.KJV: 6:7 If thou wouldst get a friend, proue him first, and be not hasty to credit him.

text.Base: For there are friends who are such when it suits them, but they will not stand by you in time of trouble.
text.KJV: 6:8 For some man is a friend for his owne occasion, and will not abide in the day of thy trouble.

text.Base: Separate yourself from your enemies, and be on the watch where your friends are concerned.
text.KJV: 6:13 Separate thy selfe from thine enemies, and take heed of thy friends.

text.Base: Faithful friends are a sturdy shelter; whoever finds one has found a treasure.
text.KJV: 6:14 A faithfull friend is a strong defence: and hee that hath found such an one, hath found a treasure.

text.Base: Faithful friends are beyond price; no amount can be weighed out against their worth.
text.KJV: 6:15 Nothing doeth counteruaile a faithful friend, and his excellencie is vnualuable.

text.Base: Faithful friends are life-saving medicine; and those who fear the Lord will find them.
text.KJV: 6:16 A faithfull friend is the medicine of life, and they that feare the Lord shal finde him.

text.Base: My child, from your youth choose discipline, and when you have gray hair you will still find wisdom.
text.KJV: 6:18 My sonne, gather instruction from thy youth vp: so shalt thou finde wisedome till thine old age.

text.Base: Come to her like one who plows and sows, and wait for her good harvest.
text.KJV: 6:19 Come vnto her as one that ploweth, and soweth, and wait for her good fruits, for thou shalt not toile much in labouring about her, but thou shalt eat of her fruits right soone.

text.Base: Search her out and seek her, and she will become known to you; and when you get hold of her, do not let her go.
text.KJV: 6:27 Search and seeke, and shee shall bee made knowen vnto thee, and when thou hast got hold of her, let her not goe.

text.Base: For at last you will find the rest she gives, and she will be changed into joy for you.
text.KJV: 6:28 For at the last thou shalt finde her rest, and that shalbe turned to thy ioy.

text.Base: If you see an intelligent person, rise early to visit him; let your foot wear out his doorstep.
text.KJV: 6:36 And if thou seest a man of vnderstanding, get thee betimes vnto him, and let thy foote weare the steps of his doore.

text.Base: Reflect on the statutes of the Lord, and meditate at all times on his commandments. It is he who will give insight to your mind, and your desire for wisdom will be granted.
text.KJV: 6:37 Let thy minde be vpon the ordinances of the Lord, & meditate continually in his commandements: he shal establish thine heart, and giue thee wisedome at thine owne desire.

text.Base: Do no evil, and evil will never overtake you.
text.KJV: 7:1 Doe no euill, so shall no harme come vnto thee.

text.Base: Stay away from wrong, and it will turn away from you.
text.KJV: 7:2 Depart from the vniust, and iniquitie shall turne away from thee.

text.Base: Do not sow in the furrows of injustice, or you may reap a sevenfold harvest.
text.KJV: 7:3 My sonne, sow not vpon the furrowes of vnrighteousnesse, and thou shalt not reape them seuen folde.

text.Base: Do not seek to become a judge, or you may be unable to root out injustice;
text.KJV: 7:6 Seeke not to be iudge, being not able to take away iniquitie, lest at any time thou feare the person of the mightie, and lay a stumbling blocke in the way of thy vprightnesse.

text.Base: Do not commit a sin twice, for not even once will you go unpunished.
text.KJV: 7:8 Bind not one sinne vpon another, for in one thou shalt not be vnpunished.

text.Base: Do not grow weary in prayer; do not neglect to give alms.
text.KJV: 7:10 Be not faint hearted when thou makest thy prayer, and neglect not to giue almes.

text.Base: Do not ridicule a person who is embittered in spirit, for there is One who humbles and exalts.
text.KJV: 7:11 Laugh no man to scorne in the bitternesse of his soule: for there is one which humbleth and exalteth.

text.Base: Do not prattle in the assembly of the elders, and do not repeat yourself in your prayer.
text.KJV: 7:14 Use not many words in a multitude of Elders, and make not much babling when thou prayest.

text.Base: Do not hate hard-working labor or farm work, which was created by the Most High.
text.KJV: 7:15 Hate not laborious worke, neither husbandrie, which the most High hath ordeined.

text.Base: Do not exchange a friend for money, or a real brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.KJV: 7:18 Change not a friend for any good by no meanes: neither a faithfull brother for the gold of Ophir.

text.Base: Do not miss out on a wise and good wife, for her charm is worth more than gold.
text.KJV: 7:19 Forgoe not a wise and good woman: for her grace is aboue gold.

text.Base: Love a wise slave like yourself; do not withhold from him his freedom.
text.KJV: 7:21 Let thy soule loue a good seruant, and defraud him not of liberty.

text.Base: If you have children, discipline them, and make them obedient from their youth.
text.KJV: 7:23 Hast thou children? instruct them, and bow downe their necke from their youth.

text.Base: With all your heart honor your father, and do not forget the birth pangs of your mother.
text.KJV: 7:27 Honour thy father with thy whole heart, and forget not the sorrowes of thy mother.

text.Base: Remember that it was of your parents you were born; how can you repay what they have given to you?
text.KJV: 7:28 Remember that thou wast begot of them, and how canst thou recompense them the things that they haue done for thee?

text.Base: Stretch out your hand to the poor, so that your blessing may be complete.
text.KJV: 7:32 And stretch thine hand vnto the poore, that thy blessing may be perfected

text.Base: Give graciously to all the living; do not withhold your kindness even from the dead.
text.KJV: 7:33 A gift hath grace in the sight of euery man liuing, and for the dead deteine it not.

text.Base: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.KJV: 7:34 Faile not to bee with them that weepe, and mourne with them that mourne.

text.Base: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such deeds you will be loved.
text.KJV: 7:35 Be not slow to visit the sicke: for that shall make thee to be beloued.

text.Base: In all you do, remember the end of your life, and then you will never sin.
text.KJV: 7:36 Whatsoeuer thou takest in hand, remember the end, and thou shalt neuer doe amisse.

text.Base: Do not contend with a powerful person, or you may fall into his hands.
text.KJV: 8:1 Striue not with a mighty man, lest thou fall into his hands.

text.Base: Do not quarrel with the rich, in case their resources outweigh yours; for gold has ruined many, and has perverted the minds of kings.
text.KJV: 8:2 Bee not at variance with a rich man, lest he ouerweigh thee: for gold hath destroyed many, and peruerted the hearts of kings.

text.Base: Do not argue with a loud-mouthed person, and do not heap wood on his fire.
text.KJV: 8:3 Striue not with a man that is full of tongue, and heape not wood vpon his fire.

text.Base: Do not reproach one who is turning away from sin; remember that we all are deserving of punishment.
text.KJV: 8:5 Reproch not a man that turneth from sinne, but remember that we are all worthy of punishment.

text.Base: Do not dishonor an old person, for some of us are growing old.
text.KJV: 8:6 Dishonour not a man in his old age: for euen some of vs waxe old.

text.Base: Do not rejoice over anyone’s death; remember that we all must die.
text.KJV: 8:7 Reioice not ouer thy greatest enemie being dead, but remember that we die all.

text.Base: Do not slight the discourse of the sages, but busy yourself with their maxims; because from them you will learn discipline and how to serve princes.
text.KJV: 8:8 Despise not the discourse of the wise, but acquaint thy selfe with their prouerbs; for of them thou shalt learne instruction, & how to serue great men with ease.

text.Base: Do not reject the tradition of the elders, which they learned from their ancestors; for from it you will learn how to understand and how to give an answer in time of need.
text.KJV: 8:9 Misse not the discourse of the Elders: for they also learned of their fathers, and of them thou shalt learne vnderstanding, and to giue answere as need requireth.

text.Base: Do not kindle the coals of a sinner, or you may be burned in his flaming fire.
text.KJV: 8:10 Kindle not the coales of a sinner, lest thou be burnt with the flame of his fire.

text.Base: Do not travel with a reckless person, or he will be a burden to you; for he will do as he pleases, and through his folly you will perish with him.
text.KJV: 8:15 Trauaile not by the way with a bold fellow, least he become grieuous vnto thee: for he will doe according to his owne will, and thou shalt perish with him through his folly.

text.Base: Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, or you will teach her an evil lesson to your own hurt.
text.KJV: 9:1 Be not iealous ouer the wife of thy bosome, and teach her not an euil lesson against thy selfe.

text.Base: Do not give yourself to a woman and let her trample down your strength.
text.KJV: 9:2 Giue not thy soule vnto a woman, to set her foot vpon thy substance.

text.Base: Do not go near a loose woman, or you will fall into her snares.
text.KJV: 9:3 Meete not with an harlot, least thou fall into her snares.

text.Base: Turn away your eyes from a shapely woman, and do not gaze at beauty belonging to another; many have been seduced by a woman’s beauty, and by it passion is kindled like a fire.
text.KJV: 9:8 Turne away thine eye from a beautifull woman, and looke not vpon anothers beautie: for many haue beene deceiued by the beautie of a woman, for heerewith loue is kindled as a fire.

text.Base: Do not abandon old friends, for new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine; when it has aged, you can drink it with pleasure.
text.KJV: 9:10 Forsake not an old friend, for the new is not comparable to him: a new friend is as new wine: when it is old, thou shalt drinke it with pleasure.

text.Base: Do not envy the success of sinners, for you do not know what their end will be.
text.KJV: 9:11 Enuy not the glory of a sinner: for thou knowest not what shall be his end.

text.Base: Keep far from those who have power to kill, and you will not be haunted by the fear of death.
text.KJV: 9:13 Keepe thee farre from the man that hath power to kill, so shalt thou not doubt the feare of death: and if thou come vnto him, make no fault, least he take away thy life presently: remember that thou goest in the midst of snares, and that thou walkest vpon the battlements of the citie.

text.Base: Reason with the intelligent, and let all your argument be about the law of the Most High.
text.KJV: 9:15 Let thy talke be with the wise, and all thy communication in the law of the most high.

text.Base: A work is praised for the skill of the artisan, so a people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.KJV: 9:17 For the hand of the artificer, the worke shall be commended: and the wise ruler of the people, for his speech.

text.Base: A wise magistrate educates his people, and the government of an intelligent person is well ordered.
text.KJV: 10:1 A wise iudge will instruct his people, & the gouernement of a prudent man is well ordered.

text.Base: As the people’s judge is, so are his officials; and as the ruler of the city is, so are all its inhabitants.
text.KJV: 10:2 As the iudge of the people is himselfe, so are his officers, and what maner of man the ruler of the citie is, such are all they that dwell therein.

text.Base: An untrained king ruins his people, but a city prospers through the intelligence of its rulers.
text.KJV: 10:3 An vnwise king destroyeth his people, but through the prudence of them which are in authoritie, the citie shalbe inhabited.

text.Base: The government of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and over it he will raise up the right leader for the time.
text.KJV: 10:4 The power of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and in due time hee will set ouer it one that is profitable.

text.Base: Arrogance is hateful to the Lord and to mortals, and injustice is outrageous to both.
text.KJV: 10:7 Pride is hatefull before God, and man: and by both doeth one commit iniquitie.

text.Base: Sovereignty passes from nation to nation because of injustice and insolence and wealth.
text.KJV: 10:8 Because of vnrighteous dealings, iniuries, and riches got by deceit, the kingdome is translated from one people to another.

text.Base: How can dust and ashes be proud? Even in life the human body decays.
text.KJV: 10:9 Why is earth and ashes proude? There is not a more wicked thing, then a couetous man: for such an one setteth his owne soule to sale, because while he liueth, he casteth away his bowels.

text.Base: The beginning of human pride is to forsake the Lord; the heart has withdrawn from its Maker.
text.KJV: 10:12 The beginning of pride is, when one departeth from God, and his heart is turned away from his maker.

text.Base: The Lord overthrows the thrones of rulers, and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.KJV: 10:14 The Lord hath cast downe the thrones of proud Princes, and set vp the meeke in their stead.

text.Base: Pride was not created for human beings, or violent anger for those born of women.
text.KJV: 10:18 Pride was not made for men, nor furious anger for them that are borne of a woman.

text.Base: The great, the judge, and the ruler are honored, but none of them is greater than the one who fears the Lord.
text.KJV: 10:24 Great men, and Iudges, and Potentates shall bee honoured, yet is there none of them greater then he that feareth the Lord.

text.Base: Better are those who work and have an abundance of everything, than those who go about boasting of their rank but lack food.
text.KJV: 10:27 Better is he that laboureth and aboundeth in all things, then hee that boasteth himselfe, and wanteth bread.

text.Base: My child, honor yourself with humility, and give yourself the esteem you deserve.
text.KJV: 10:28 My sonne, glorifie thy soule in meekenesse, and giue it honour according to the dignitie thereof.

text.Base: The wisdom of the lowly lifts up their heads, and seats them among the great.
text.KJV: 11:1 Wisedome lifteth vp the head of him that is of low degree, and maketh him to sit among great men.

text.Base: Do not praise individuals for their good looks, or loathe anyone because of appearance.
text.KJV: 11:2 Commend not a man for his beautie, neither abhorre a man for his outward appearance.

text.Base: The bee is small among flying creatures, but her product is the best of sweet things.
text.KJV: 11:3 The Bee is little among such as flie, but her fruite is the chiefe of sweete things.

text.Base: Do not boast about your clothing, or lift yourself up on the day you are honored; for the works of the Lord are wonderful, and his activities are hidden from mortals.
text.KJV: 11:4 Boast not of thy cloathing and raiment, and exalt not thy selfe in the day of honour: for the workes of the Lord are wonderfull, and his workes among men are hidden.

text.Base: Do not find fault before you investigate; examine first, and then criticize.
text.KJV: 11:7 Blame not before thou hast examined the trueth: vnderstand first, and then rebuke.

text.Base: Do not answer before you listen, and do not interrupt when another is speaking.
text.KJV: 11:8 Answere not, before thou hast heard the cause: neither interrupt men in the midst of their talke.

text.Base: Do not argue about a matter that does not concern you, and do not sit with sinners when they judge a case.
text.KJV: 11:9 Striue not in a matter that concerneth thee not: and sit not in iudgement with sinners.

text.Base: My child, do not busy yourself with many matters; if you multiply activities, you will not go unpunished.
text.KJV: 11:10 My sonne, meddle not with many matters: for if thou meddle much, thou shalt not be innocent: and if thou follow after, thou shalt not obtaine, neither shalt thou escape by flying.

text.Base: Good things and bad, life and death, poverty and wealth, come from the Lord.
text.KJV: 11:14 Prosperitie and aduersitie, life and death, pouerty and riches, come of the Lord.

text.Base: Do not wonder at the works of a sinner, but trust in the Lord and keep at your job; for it is easy in the sight of the Lord to make the poor rich suddenly, in a moment.
text.KJV: 11:21 Marueile not at the workes of sinners, but trust in the Lord, and abide in thy labour: for it is an easie thing in the sight of the Lord, on the sudden to make a poore man rich.

text.Base: It is easy for the Lord on the day of death to reward individuals according to their conduct.
text.KJV: 11:26 For it is an easie thing vnto the Lord in the day of death, to reward a man according to his wayes.

text.Base: Call no one happy before his death; by how he ends, a person becomes known.
text.KJV: 11:28 Iudge none blessed before his death: for a man shall bee knowen in his children.

text.Base: If you do good, know to whom you do it, and you will be thanked for your good deeds.
text.KJV: 12:1 When thou wilt doe good, know to whō thou doest it, so shalt thou be thanked for thy benefites.

text.Base: Do good to the devout, and you will be repaid—if not by them, certainly by the Most High.
text.KJV: 12:2 Do good to the godly man, and thou shalt find a recompence, and if not from him, yet from the most high.

text.Base: Give to the devout, but do not help the sinner.
text.KJV: 12:4 Giue to the godly man, and helpe not a sinner.

text.Base: A friend is not known in prosperity, nor is an enemy hidden in adversity.
text.KJV: 12:8 A friend cannot be knowen in prosperitie, and an enemy cannot be hidden in aduersitie.

text.Base: Never trust your enemy, for like corrosion in copper, so is his wickedness.
text.KJV: 12:10 Neuer trust thine enemie: for like as yron rusteth, so is his wickednesse.

text.Base: Who pities a snake charmer when he is bitten, or all those who go near wild animals?
text.KJV: 12:13 Who will pitie a charmer that is bitten with a serpent, or any such as come nigh wilde beasts?

text.Base: So no one pities a person who associates with a sinner and becomes involved in his sins.
text.KJV: 12:14 So one that goeth to a sinner, and is defiled with him in his sinnes, who will pitie?

text.Base: An enemy has sweetness on his lips, but in his heart he plans to throw you into a pit; an enemy may have tears in his eyes, but if he finds an opportunity he will never have enough of your blood.
text.KJV: 12:16 An enemie speaketh sweetly with his lippes, but in his heart he imagineth how to throw thee into a pit: hee will weepe with his eyes, but if he find opportunitie, hee will not be satisfied with blood.

text.Base: Whoever touches pitch will be defiled, and whoever associates with the proud will become like them.
text.KJV: 13:1 He that toucheth pitch, shal be defiled therewith, and hee that hath fellowship with a proude man, shall be like vnto him.

text.Base: Do not lift a weight beyond your strength, or associate with one who is mightier and richer than you.
text.KJV: 13:2 Burthen not thy selfe aboue thy power, while thou liuest, and haue no fellowship with one that is mightier, and richer then thy selfe. For how agree the kettle and the earthen pot together? for if the one be smitten against the other, it shall be broken.

text.Base: A rich person does a wrong, and even adds insults; a poor person suffers a wrong, and must add apologies.
text.KJV: 13:3 The rich man hath done wrong, and yet he threatneth withall: the poore is wronged, and he must intreat also.

text.Base: Do not push forward, or you may be repulsed; and do not keep too far away, or you may be forgotten.
text.KJV: 13:10 Presse thou not vpon him, lest thou be put backe, stand not farre off, lest thou be forgotten.

text.Base: Every creature loves its like, and every person his neighbor.
text.KJV: 13:15 Euery beast loueth his like, and euery man loueth his neighbour.

text.Base: All living beings associate with their own kind, and people stick to those like themselves.
text.KJV: 13:16 All flesh consorteth according to kind, and a man will cleaue to his like:

text.Base: What peace is there between a hyena and a dog? And what peace between the rich and the poor?
text.KJV: 13:18 What agreement is there betweene the Hyena and a dogge? and what peace betweene the rich and the poore?

text.Base: Wild asses in the wilderness are the prey of lions; likewise the poor are feeding grounds for the rich.
text.KJV: 13:19 As the wilde asse is the lyons pray in the wildernesse: so the rich eate vp the poore.

text.Base: Wealth is good if it is free from sin; poverty is evil only in the opinion of the ungodly.
text.KJV: 13:24 Riches are good vnto him that hath no sinne, and pouerty is euill in the mouth of the vngodly.

text.Base: The heart changes the face, either for good or for evil.
text.KJV: 13:25 The heart of a man changeth his countenance, whether it be for good or euill: and a merry heart maketh a cheerefull countenance.

text.Base: Happy are those who do not blunder with their lips, and need not suffer remorse for sin.
text.KJV: 14:1 Blessed is the man that hath not slipt with his mouth, and is not pricked with the multitude of sinnes.

text.Base: Happy are those whose hearts do not condemn them, and who have not given up their hope.
text.KJV: 14:2 Blessed is hee whose conscience hath not condemned him, and who is not fallen from his hope in the Lord.

text.Base: Riches are not appropriate for a small-minded person; and of what use is wealth to a miser?
text.KJV: 14:3 Riches are not comely for a niggard: and what should an enuious man doe with money?

text.Base: He who is evil to himself, to whom will he be good? He will not enjoy his own riches.
text.KJV: 14:5 Hee that is euill to himselfe, to whom will he be good? he shall not take pleasure in his goods.

text.Base: My child, if you have anything, do good to yourself, and bring worthy offerings to the Lord.
text.KJV: 14:11 My sonne, according to thy habilitie doe good to thy selfe, and giue the Lord his due offering.

text.Base: Remember that death does not tarry, and the decree of Hades has not been shown to you.
text.KJV: 14:12 Remember that death will not be long in comming, and that the couenant of the graue is not shewed vnto thee.

text.Base: Do good to friends before you die, and reach out and give to them as much as you can.
text.KJV: 14:13 Doe good vnto thy friend before thou die, and according to thy abilitie, stretch out thy hand and giue to him.

text.Base: All living beings grow old like a garment; for the decree from of old is, “You must surely die!”
text.KJV: 14:17 All flesh waxeth old as a garment: for the couenant from the beginning is; thou shalt die the death.

text.Base: Like abundant leaves on a spreading tree that sheds some and puts forth others, so are the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another is born.
text.KJV: 14:18 As of the greene leaues on a thicke tree, some fall, and some grow; so is the generation of flesh and blood, one commeth to an end, and another is borne.

text.Base: Happy is the person who meditates on wisdom and reasons with intelligent understanding,
text.KJV: 14:20 Blessed is the man that doeth meditate good things in wisdome, and that reasoneth of holy things by his vnderstanding.

text.Base: who pursues her like a hunter, and lies in wait on her paths;
text.KJV: 14:22 Goe after her as one that traceth, and lie in wait in her wayes.

text.Base: he will place his children under her shelter, and will dwell under her branches;
text.KJV: 14:26 He shal set his children vnder her shelter, and shall lodge vnder her branches.

text.Base: he will be sheltered by her from the heat, and will dwell in the midst of her glory.
text.KJV: 14:27 By her he shall be couered from heat, and in her glory shall he dwell.

text.Base: Whoever fears the Lord will do this, and whoever holds to the law will obtain wisdom.
text.KJV: 15:1 He that feareth the Lord will doe good, and he that hath the knowledge of the Law shal obtaine her.

text.Base: She will come to meet him like a mother, and like a young bride she will welcome him.
text.KJV: 15:2 And as a mother shall she meet him, and receiue him as a wife maried of a virgin.

text.Base: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.
text.KJV: 15:3 With the bread of vnderstanding shall she feed him, and giue him the water of wisedome to drinke.

text.Base: He will find gladness and a crown of rejoicing, and will acquire an everlasting name.
text.KJV: 15:6 He shall finde ioy, and a crowne of gladnesse, and she shall cause him to inherit an euerlasting name.

text.Base: Do not say, “It was the Lord’s doing that I fell away”; for he does not do what he hates.
text.KJV: 15:11 Say not thou, It is through the Lord, that I fell away, for thou oughtest not to doe the things that he hateth.

text.Base: Do not say, “It was he who led me astray”; for he has no need of the sinful.
text.KJV: 15:12 Say not thou, He hath caused mee to erre, for hee hath no need of the sinfull man.

text.Base: The Lord hates all abominations; such things are not loved by those who fear him.
text.KJV: 15:13 The Lord hateth all abomination, and they that feare God loue it not.

text.Base: It was he who created humankind in the beginning, and he left them in the power of their own free choice.
text.KJV: 15:14 Hee himselfe made man from the beginning, and left him in the hand of his counsell,

text.Base: If you choose, you can keep the commandments, and to act faithfully is a matter of your own choice.
text.KJV: 15:15 If thou wilt, to keepe the Commandements, and to performe acceptable faithfulnesse.

text.Base: He has placed before you fire and water; stretch out your hand for whichever you choose.
text.KJV: 15:16 He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thy hand vnto whether thou wilt.

text.Base: Before each person are life and death, and whichever one chooses will be given.
text.KJV: 15:17 Before man is life and death, and whether him liketh shalbe giuen him.

text.Base: For great is the wisdom of the Lord; he is mighty in power and sees everything;
text.KJV: 15:18 For the wisedome of the Lord is great, and he is mighty in power, and beholdeth all things,

text.Base: his eyes are on those who fear him, and he knows every human action.
text.KJV: 15:19 And his eyes are vpon them that feare him, & hee knoweth euery worke of man.

text.Base: He has not commanded anyone to be wicked, and he has not given anyone permission to sin.
text.KJV: 15:20 Hee hath commanded no man to do wickedly, neither hath he giuen any man license to sinne.

text.Base: Do not desire a multitude of worthless children, and do not rejoice in ungodly offspring.
text.KJV: 16:1 Desire not a multitude of vnprofitable children, neither delight in vngodly sonnes.

text.Base: If they multiply, do not rejoice in them, unless the fear of the Lord is in them.
text.KJV: 16:2 Though they multiply, reioyce not in them, except the feare of the Lord be with them.

text.Base: In a gathering of sinners a fire is kindled, and in a disobedient nation wrath blazes up.
text.KJV: 16:6 In the congregation of the vngodly, shall a fire be kindled, and in a rebellious nation, wrath is set on fire.

text.Base: He did not forgive the ancient giants who revolted in their might.
text.KJV: 16:7 Hee was not pacified towards the olde giants, who fell away in the strength of their foolishnesse.

text.Base: And if there is even one stubborn person, it is a wonder if he could go unpunished. For mercy and wrath are with the Lord; he is mighty to forgive, and he pours out wrath.
text.KJV: 16:11 And if there be one stiffe-necked among the people, it is marueile, if he escape vnpunished; for mercy and wrath are with him, hee is mighty to forgiue, and to powre out displeasure.

text.Base: As great as is his mercy, so also is his chastisement; he judges a person according to one’s deeds.
text.KJV: 16:12 As his mercy is great, so is his correction also: he iudgeth a man according to his workes.

text.Base: Do not say, “I am hidden from the Lord, and who from on high has me in mind? Among so many people I am unknown, for what am I in a boundless creation?”
text.KJV: 16:17 Say not thou, I will hide my selfe from the Lord: shall any remember me from aboue? I shall not be remembred among so many people: for what is my soule among such an infinite number of creatures?

text.Base: Behold, heaven and the highest heaven, the abyss and the earth, tremble at his visitation!
text.KJV: 16:18 Behold, the heauen, and the heauen of heauens, the deepe and the earth, and all that therein is, shall be mooued when he shall visit.

text.Base: When the Lord created his works from the beginning, and, in making them, determined their boundaries,
text.KJV: 16:26 The works of the Lord are done in iudgement from the beginning: and from the time he made them, hee disposed the parts thereof.

text.Base: he arranged his works in an eternal order, and their dominion for all generations of them. They neither hunger nor grow weary, and they do not abandon their tasks.
text.KJV: 16:27 Hee garnished his workes for euer, and in his hand are the chiefe of them vnto all generations: they neither labour, nor are weary, nor cease from their workes.

text.Base: The Lord created human beings out of earth, and makes them return to it again.
text.KJV: 17:1 The Lord created man of the earth, and turned him into it againe.

text.Base: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his own image.
text.KJV: 17:3 He endued them with strength by themselues, and made them according to his image,

text.Base: He filled them with knowledge and understanding, and showed them good and evil.
text.KJV: 17:7 Withall, hee filled them with the knowledge of vnderstanding, & shewed them good and euill.

text.Base: He bestowed upon them knowledge, and gave them the law of life as a heritage.
text.KJV: 17:11 Beside this he gaue them knowledge, and the law of life for an heritage.

text.Base: He established with them an eternal covenant, and revealed to them his decrees.
text.KJV: 17:12 He made an euerlasting couenant with them, and shewed them his iudgements.

text.Base: Their ways are always before him; they will not be hidden from his eyes.
text.KJV: 17:15 Their wayes are euer before him, and shall not be hid from his eyes.

text.Base: All their works are as the sun before him, and his eyes are continually upon their ways.
text.KJV: 17:19 Therefore all their workes are as the Sunne before him, and his eyes are continually vpon their wayes.

text.Base: But to those who repent he grants a return, and he encourages those who are losing their hope.
text.KJV: 17:24 But vnto them that repent, he granted them returne, and comforted those that faile in patience.

text.Base: Turn to the Lord and forsake your sins; pray in his presence and lessen your offense.
text.KJV: 17:25 Returne vnto the Lord, and forsake thy sinnes, make thy prayer before his face, and offend lesse.

text.Base: Return to the Most High and turn away from iniquity, and hate intensely what he abhors.
text.KJV: 17:26 Turne againe to the most High, and turne away from iniquitie: for he will leade thee out of darkenesse into the light of health, and hate thou abomination vehemently.

text.Base: How great is the mercy of the Lord, and his forgiveness for those who return to him!
text.KJV: 17:28 Thankesgiuing perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not: the liuing and sound in heart, shall praise the Lord.

text.Base: He who lives forever created the whole universe.
text.KJV: 18:1 Hee that liueth for euer, created all things in generall.

text.Base: What are mortals, and of what use are they? What is their good and what is their evil?
text.KJV: 18:8 What is man, and whereto serueth he? what is his good, & what is his euil?

text.Base: The number of their days is great if they reach a hundred years.
text.KJV: 18:9 The number of a mans dayes at the most are an hundred yeeres.

text.Base: Like a drop of water from the sea and a grain of sand, so are a few years among the days of eternity.
text.KJV: 18:10 As a drop of water vnto the Sea, and a grauell stone in comparison of the sand, so are a thousand yeeres to the dayes of eternitie.

text.Base: Therefore the Lord is patient with them and pours out his mercy upon them.
text.KJV: 18:11 Therfore is God patient with them, & powreth forth his mercy vpon them.

text.Base: The compassion of human beings is for their neighbors, but the compassion of the Lord is for every living thing.
text.KJV: 18:13 The mercy of man is toward his neighbour, but the mercy of the Lord is vpon all flesh: he reprooueth and nurtureth, and teacheth, & bringeth againe as a shepheard his flocke.

text.Base: My child, do not mix reproach with your good deeds, or bitter words with any gift.
text.KJV: 18:15 My sonne, blemish not thy good deeds, neither vse vncomfortable words when thou giuest any thing.

text.Base: Before you speak, learn; and before you fall sick, take care of yourself.
text.KJV: 18:19 Learne before thou speake, and vse phisicke, or euer thou be sicke.

text.Base: Before judgment comes, examine yourself; and at the time of scrutiny you will find forgiveness.
text.KJV: 18:20 Before iudgement examine thy selfe, and in the day of visitation thou shalt find mercy.

text.Base: Do not follow your base desires, but restrain your appetites.
text.KJV: 18:30 Goe not after thy lustes, but refraine thy selfe from thine appetites.

text.Base: A worker who is a drunkard will never become rich; one who despises small things will fail little by little.
text.KJV: 19:1 A labouring man that is giuen to drunkennesse shal not be rich, and hee that contemneth small things shall fall by little & little.

text.Base: Wine and women lead intelligent men astray, and the man who consorts with prostitutes is reckless.
text.KJV: 19:2 Wine and women will make men of vnderstanding to fall away, and he that cleaueth to harlots will become impudent.

text.Base: One who trusts others too quickly is light-minded, and one who sins does wrong to himself.
text.KJV: 19:4 He that is hasty to giue credit is light minded, and he that sinneth shall offend against his owne soule.

text.Base: Question a friend; perhaps he did not do it; or if he did, so that he may not do it again.
text.KJV: 19:13 Admonish a friend, it may be he hath not done it, and if he haue that he doe it no more.

text.Base: Question a neighbor; perhaps he did not say it; or if he said it, so that he may not repeat it.
text.KJV: 19:14 Admonish thy friend, it may be he hath not said it, and if he haue, that he speake it not againe.

text.Base: Question a friend, for often it is only slander; do not believe everything you hear.
text.KJV: 19:15 Admonish a friend: for many times it is a slander, & beleeue not euery tale.

text.Base: All wisdom is the fear of the Lord, and in all wisdom there is the fulfillment of the law.
text.KJV: 19:20 The feare of the Lord is all wisedome, and in all wisedome is the performance of the Law, and the knowledge of his omnipotencie.

text.Base: Better are the God-fearing who lack understanding than the highly intelligent who transgress the law.
text.KJV: 19:24 He that hath smal vnderstanding and feareth God, is better then one that hath much wisedome, and transgresseth the Law of the most High.

text.Base: There is a rebuke that is untimely, and there is the person who is wise enough to keep silent.
text.KJV: 20:1 There is a reproofe that is not comely: againe some man holdeth his tongue, and he is wise.

text.Base: How much better it is to rebuke than to fume!
text.KJV: 20:2 It is much better to reprooue, then to be angry secretly, and he that confesseth his fault, shall be preserued from hurt.

text.Base: One person keeps silent and is found to be wise; another is detested for being talkative.
text.KJV: 20:5 There is one that keepeth silence and is found wise: and another by much babling becommeth hatefull.

text.Base: A wise person will keep silent until the right moment, but a boasting fool misses the right moment.
text.KJV: 20:7 A wise man wil hold his tongue till he see opportunitie: but a babler and a foole will regard no time.

text.Base: A slip on the pavement is better than a slip of the tongue; so the downfall of the wicked will come speedily.
text.KJV: 20:18 To slip vpon a pauement, is better then to slip with the tongue: so, the fall of the wicked shall come speedily.

text.Base: A lie is a foul blot on a person; it is continually on the mouth of the ignorant.
text.KJV: 20:24 A lie is a foule blot in a man, yet it is continually in the mouth of the vntaught.

text.Base: The wise person advances himself by his words, and one who is sensible pleases the great.
text.KJV: 20:27 A wise man shall promote himselfe to honour with his words: and hee that hath vnderstanding, will please great men.

text.Base: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure, of what value is either?
text.KJV: 20:30 Wisedome that is hidde, and treasure that is hoarded vp, what profit is in them both?

text.Base: Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom.
text.KJV: 20:31 Better is he that hideth his folly, then a man that hideth his wisedome.

text.Base: Have you sinned, my child? Do so no more, but ask forgiveness for your past sins.
text.KJV: 21:1 My sonne, hast thou sinned? doe so no more, but aske pardon for thy former sinnes.

text.Base: Flee from sin as from a snake; for if you approach it, it will bite you; its teeth are lion’s teeth, and can destroy human lives.
text.KJV: 21:2 Flee from sinne as from the face of a Serpent: for if thou commest too neere it, it will bite thee: the teeth thereof, are as the teeth of a lyon, slaying the soules of men.

text.Base: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; there is no healing for the wound it inflicts.
text.KJV: 21:3 All iniquitie is as a two edged sword, the wounds whereof cannot be healed.

text.Base: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgment comes speedily.
text.KJV: 21:5 A prayer out of a poore mans mouth reacheth to the eares of God, and his iudgement commeth speedily.

text.Base: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.
text.KJV: 21:10 The way of sinners is made plaine with stones, but at the ende thereof is the pit of hell.

text.Base: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it;
text.KJV: 21:15 If a skilfull man heare a wise word, hee will commend it, and adde vnto it: but assoone as one of no vnderstanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behinde his backe.

text.Base: The chatter of a fool is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.
text.KJV: 21:16 The talking of a foole is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.

text.Base: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but a clever person smiles quietly.
text.KJV: 21:20 A foole lifteth vp his voyce with laughter, but a wise man doeth scarce smile a litle.

text.Base: The heart of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.KJV: 21:26 The heart of fooles is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.

text.Base: The sluggard is like a filthy stone, and everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.KJV: 22:1 A slouthful man is compared to a filthy stone, and euery one will hisse him out to his disgrace.

text.Base: It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.
text.KJV: 22:3 An euill nurtured sonne is the dishonour of his father that begate him: and a daughter is borne to his losse.

text.Base: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.
text.KJV: 22:4 A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but shee that liueth dishonestly, is her fathers heauinesse.

text.Base: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.
text.KJV: 22:7 Who so teacheth a foole, is as one that gleweth a potsheard together, and as hee that waketh one from a sound sleepe.

text.Base: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind.
text.KJV: 22:11 Weepe for the dead, for hee hath lost the light: and weepe for the foole, for he wanteth vnderstanding: make litle weeping for the dead, for hee is at rest: but the life of the foole is worse then death.

text.Base: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for a fool or an ungodly person it lasts all the days of their lives.
text.KJV: 22:12 Seuen dayes doe men mourne for him that is dead; but for a foole, and an vngodly man, all the dayes of his life.

text.Base: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow—in these cases any friend will flee.
text.KJV: 22:22 If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, feare not, for there may be a reconciliation: except for vpbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound, for, for these things euery friend will depart.

text.Base: Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?
text.KJV: 22:27 Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seale of wisedome vpon my lippes, that I fall not suddenly by them, & that my tongue destroy me not?

text.Base: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!
text.KJV: 23:1 O Lord, father and gouernour of all my whole life, leaue me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.

text.Base: Listen, my children, to instruction about the mouth; the one who keeps it will not be caught.
text.KJV: 23:7 Heare, O yee children, the discipline of the mouth: He that keepeth it, shall neuer be taken in his lippes.

text.Base: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One.
text.KJV: 23:9 Accustome not thy mouth to swearing: neither vse thy selfe to the naming of the holy one.

text.Base: A man who violates his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me; why should I fear? The Most High will not remember my sins.”
text.KJV: 23:18 A man that breaketh wedlocke, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darknesse: the walles couer me; & no body seeth me, what neede I to feare? The most high wil not remember my sinnes:

text.Base: His fear is confined to human eyes, and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun.
text.KJV: 23:19 Such a man only feareth the eies of men, and knoweth not that the eies of the Lord are tenne thousand times brighter then the Sunne, beholding all the waies of men, and considering the most secret parts.

text.Base: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.
text.KJV: 23:22 Thus shall it goe also with the wife, that leaueth her husband, and bringeth in an heire by another:

text.Base: Those who survive will know that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the Lord’s commandments.
text.KJV: 23:27 And they that remaine, shall know that there is nothing better then the feare of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter then to take heed vnto the Commandement of the Lord.

text.Base: Wisdom praises herself, and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.
text.KJV: 24:1 Wisedome shall praise her selfe, and shall glory in the midst of her people.

text.Base: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:
text.KJV: 24:2 In the Congregation of the most high, shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.

text.Base: “I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.”
text.KJV: 24:3 I came out of the mouth of the most High, and couered the earth as a cloud.

text.Base: “Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, ‘Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.’”
text.KJV: 24:8 So the creatour of all things gaue mee a commandement, and hee that made me, caused my tabernacle to rest: and said, Let thy dwelling be in Iacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.

text.Base: “Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.”
text.KJV: 24:9 Hee created mee from the beginning before the world, and I shall neuer faile.

text.Base: “Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.”
text.KJV: 24:19 Come vnto me all ye that be desirous of mee, and fill your selues with my fruits.

text.Base: “For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.”
text.KJV: 24:20 For my memorial is sweeter then hony, and mine inheritance then the hony combe.

text.Base: “Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.”
text.KJV: 24:21 They that eate mee shall yet be hungry, and they that drinke me shall yet be thirstie.

text.Base: All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.
text.KJV: 24:23 All these things are the booke of the Couenant of the most high God, euen the Law which Moses commanded for an heritage vnto the Congregations of Iacob.

text.Base: I take pleasure in three things, and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband who live in harmony.
text.KJV: 25:1 In three things I was beautified, and stoode vp beautiful, both before God and men: the vnitie of brethren, the loue of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.

text.Base: I detest three kinds of people... a beggar who is proud, a rich man who is a liar, and an adulterous old man who lacks good sense.
text.KJV: 25:2 Three sorts of men my soule hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poore man that is proud, a rich man that is a lyar, and an olde adulterer that doteth.

text.Base: I can think of nine whom I would call blessed, and a tenth my tongue proclaims: a man who can rejoice in his children; a man who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.KJV: 25:7 There be nine things which I haue iudged in mine heart to be happy, and the tenth I will vtter with my tongue: a man that hath ioy of his children, and he that liueth to see the fall of his enemie.

text.Base: Happy is the one who lives with a sensible wife, and the one who does not plow with ox and ass together. Happy is the one who does not slip with the tongue, and the one who has not served a man inferior to himself.
text.KJV: 25:8 Well is him that dwelleth with a wife of vnderstanding, and that hath not slipped with his tongue, and that hath not serued a man more vnworthy then himselfe.

text.Base: Happy is the one who finds a friend, and the one who speaks to attentive listeners.
text.KJV: 25:9 Well is him that hath found prudence, and he that speaketh in the eares of him that will heare.

text.Base: How great is the one who finds wisdom! But none is superior to the one who fears the Lord.
text.KJV: 25:10 Oh how great is he that findeth wisedome! yet is there none aboue him that feareth the Lord.

text.Base: From a woman sin had its beginning, and because of her we all die.
text.KJV: 25:24 Of the woman came the beginning of sinne, & through her wee all die.

text.Base: Happy is the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.KJV: 26:1 Blessed is the man that hath a vertuous wife, for the number of his dayes shall be double.

text.Base: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.KJV: 26:2 A vertuous woman reioyceth her husband, and he shall fulfill the yeeres of his life in peace.

text.Base: A good wife is a great blessing; she will be granted among the blessings of the man who fears the Lord.
text.KJV: 26:3 A good wife is a good portion, which shall be giuen in the portion of them that feare the Lord.

text.Base: A wife’s charm delights her husband, and her skill puts fat on his bones.
text.KJV: 26:13 The grace of a wife delighteth her husband, and her discretion will fat his bones.

text.Base: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord, and nothing is so precious as her self-discipline.
text.KJV: 26:14 A silent and louing woman is a gift of the Lord, and there is nothing so much worth, as a mind well instructed.

text.Base: Two things grieve my heart, and a third makes me angry: a warrior in want through poverty, intelligent men who are treated with contempt, and a man who turns back from righteousness to sin—the Lord will prepare him for the sword!
text.KJV: 26:28 There be two things that grieue my heart: and the third maketh me angry: a man of warre that suffereth pouerty, and men of vnderstanding that are not set by: and one that returneth from righteousnesse to sinne: the Lord prepareth such a one for the sword.

text.Base: A merchant can hardly keep from wrongdoing, nor is a tradesman innocent of sin.
text.KJV: 26:29 A merchant shall hardly keepe himselfe from doing wrong: and an huckster shall not be freed from sinne.

text.Base: Many have sinned for the sake of profit, and one who seeks to grow rich must turn away his eyes.
text.KJV: 27:1 Many haue sinned for a smal matter: & he that seeketh for abundance will turne his eies away.

text.Base: As a stake is driven firmly into a fissure between stones, so sin is wedged in between selling and buying.
text.KJV: 27:2 As a naile sticketh fast betweene the ioynings of the stones: so doth sinne sticke close betweene buying and selling.

text.Base: Unless a person holds zealously to the fear of the Lord, his house will be quickly overthrown.
text.KJV: 27:3 Unlesse a man hold himselfe diligently in the feare of the Lord, his house shall soone be ouerthrowen.

text.Base: When a sieve is shaken, the refuse appears; so do a person’s faults when he speaks.
text.KJV: 27:4 As when one sifteth with a sieue, the refuse remaineth, so the filth of man in his talke.

text.Base: The kiln tests the potter’s vessels; so the test of a person is in his conversation.
text.KJV: 27:5 The furnace prooueth the potters vessell: so the triall of man is in his reasoning.

text.Base: Its fruit discloses the cultivation of a tree; so a person’s speech discloses the cultivation of his mind.
text.KJV: 27:6 The fruite declareth if the tree haue beene dressed: so is the vtterance of a conceit in the heart of man.

text.Base: One who discloses secrets destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.KJV: 27:16 Who so discouereth secrets, looseth his credit: and shall neuer find friend to his minde.

text.Base: Love your friend and keep faith with him; but if you disclose his secrets, do not follow after him.
text.KJV: 27:17 Loue thy friend, and be faithfull vnto him: but if thou bewrayest his secrets, follow no more after him.

text.Base: Whoever winks with the eye plots mischief, and those who know him will keep their distance.
text.KJV: 27:22 He that winketh with the eies worketh euil, and he that knoweth him will depart from him.

text.Base: The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance, for he keeps a strict account of their sins.
text.KJV: 28:1 He that reuengeth shall find vengeance from the Lord, and he will surely keepe his sinnes (in remembrance.)

text.Base: Forgive your neighbor the wrong he has done, and then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.
text.KJV: 28:2 Forgiue thy neighbour the hurt that he hath done vnto thee, so shall thy sinnes also be forgiuen when thou prayest.

text.Base: Does anyone harbor anger against another, and expect healing from the Lord?
text.KJV: 28:3 One man beareth hatred against another, and doeth he seeke pardon from the Lord?

text.Base: If one has no mercy toward another like himself, can he then beg for pardon of his own sins?
text.KJV: 28:4 Hee sheweth no mercy to a man, which is like himselfe: and doeth hee aske forgiuenesse of his owne sinnes?

text.Base: Remember the end of your life, and set enmity aside; remember corruption and death, and be true to the commandments.
text.KJV: 28:6 Remember thy end, and let enimitie cease, corruption and death, and abide in the Commandements.

text.Base: If you blow on a spark, it will glow; if you spit on it, it will be put out; yet both come out of your mouth.
text.KJV: 28:12 If thou blow the sparke, it shall burne: if thou spit vpon it, it shall bee quenched, and both these come out of thy mouth.

text.Base: Curse the whisperer and the double-tongued, for they have destroyed many who were at peace.
text.KJV: 28:13 Curse the whisperer, and double tongued: for such haue destroyed many that were at peace.

text.Base: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as have fallen because of the tongue.
text.KJV: 28:18 Many haue fallen by the edge of the sword: but not so many as haue fallen by the tongue.

text.Base: The merciful lend to their neighbors; by holding out a helping hand they keep the commandments.
text.KJV: 29:1 Hee that is mercifull, will lende vnto his neighbour, and hee that strengthneth his hande, keepeth the Commandements.

text.Base: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need; pay your neighbor back at the appointed time.
text.KJV: 29:2 Lend to thy neighbour in time of his need, and pay thou thy neighbour againe in due season.

text.Base: Be patient with someone in humble circumstances, and do not keep him waiting for your alms.
text.KJV: 29:8 Yet haue thou patience with a man in poore estate, and delay not to shew him mercy.

text.Base: Lose your silver for the sake of a brother or a friend, and do not let it rust under a stone and be lost.
text.KJV: 29:10 Lose thy money for thy brother and thy friend, and let it not rust vnder a stone to be lost.

text.Base: Lay up your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and it will profit you more than gold.
text.KJV: 29:11 Lay vp thy treasure according to the commandements of the most high, and it shall bring thee more profite then golde.

text.Base: The essentials of life are water and bread and clothing, and a house to provide privacy.
text.KJV: 29:21 The chiefe thing for life is water and bread, and clothing, and an house to couer shame.

text.Base: Better is the life of a poor person under the shelter of his own roof than sumptuous food in another’s house.
text.KJV: 29:22 Better is the life of a poore man in a meane cottage, then delicate fare in another mans house.

text.Base: To a sensible person these things are intolerable: expression of grievance from a household, and the insults of a moneylender.
text.KJV: 29:28 These things are grieuous to a man of vnderstanding: the vpbraiding of house-roome, and reproching of the lender.

text.Base: He who loves his son will whip him often, so that he may rejoice at the way he turns out.
text.KJV: 30:1 Hee that loueth his sonne, causeth him oft to feele the rodde, that hee may haue ioy of him in the end.

text.Base: He who disciplines his son will profit by him, and will boast of him among acquaintances.
text.KJV: 30:2 He that chastiseth his sonne, shall haue ioy in him, and shall reioyce of him among his acquaintance.

text.Base: A horse untamed grows stubborn, and a son left to himself grows headstrong.
text.KJV: 30:8 An horse not broken becommeth headstrong: and a childe left to himselfe will be wilfull.

text.Base: Discipline your son and make his yoke heavy, so that you may not be offended by his shamelessness.
text.KJV: 30:13 Chastise thy sonne, and hold him to labour, lest his lewd behauiour be an offence vnto thee.

text.Base: Better off is a poor man who is well and strong in constitution than a rich man who is severely afflicted in body.
text.KJV: 30:14 Better is the poore being sound and strong of constitution, then a rich man that is afflicted in his body.

text.Base: Health and vigor are better than any gold, and a robust body than countless riches.
text.KJV: 30:15 Health and good state of body are aboue all gold, and a strong body aboue infinite wealth.

text.Base: Death is better than a miserable life, and eternal rest than chronic sickness.
text.KJV: 30:17 Death is better then a bitter life, or continuall sickenesse.

text.Base: Do not give yourself over to sorrow, and do not distress yourself deliberately.
text.KJV: 30:21 Giue not ouer thy mind to heauinesse, and afflict not thy selfe in thine owne counsell.

text.Base: Beguile yourself and comfort your heart, and drive sorrow far from you, for sorrow has destroyed many, and there is no profit in it.
text.KJV: 30:23 Loue thine owne soule, and comfort thy heart, remoue sorrow far from thee: for sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit therein.

text.Base: Jealousy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.KJV: 30:24 Enuie and wrath shorten the life, and carefulnesse bringeth age before the time.

text.Base: Wakefulness over wealth wastes the flesh, and anxiety about it drives away sleep.
text.KJV: 31:1 Watching for riches, consumeth the flesh, and the care therof driueth away sleepe.

text.Base: Are you seated at a great table? Do not be greedy at it, and do not say, “How much food there is here!”
text.KJV: 31:12 If thou sit at a bountifull table, bee not greedy vpon it, and say not, There is much meate on it.

text.Base: Remember that a greedy eye is a bad thing; what has been created more greedy than an eye? Therefore it sheds tears from every face.
text.KJV: 31:13 Remember that a wicked eye is an euill thing: and what is created more wicked then an eye? therefore it weepeth vpon euery occasion.

text.Base: Eat like a human being what is set before you, and do not chew greedily, or you will be despised.
text.KJV: 31:16 Eate as it becommeth a man those things which are set before thee: and deuoure not, lest thou be hated.
